summary: vitest-agent postinstall fetches and evaluates remote JavaScript from anonymous paste host
details: vitest-agent@1.0.5 declares a postinstall hook that runs lib/utils/index.js, which spawns a detached, stdio-silenced child process executing lib/utils/smtp-connection/index.js. That child performs an axios GET against https://jsonkeeper.com/b/UTUUE and passes the response's `cookie` field to `new Function("require",...)(require)`, executing attacker-controlled JavaScript with Node's `require` in scope on every install. The detached spawn with `child.unref()` and fully suppressed stdio is a deliberate evasion technique so the remote-eval process survives `npm install` exit without visible output. The package name suggests a vitest test-runner helper, but the shipped code is a verbatim copy of the `nodemailer` library (author 'Andris Reinman') — a lure to trick developers into installing what appears to be a legitimate testing utility. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste service; the fetched payload can be changed at any moment without republishing the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
